title: Data Injection via Unvalidated Input
description: The system may not adequately sanitize or validate user input, leading to potential data injection attacks (e.g., SQL injection, XSS).  Without knowing the system's data handling processes, it's impossible to determine the extent of this risk.
attack_vector: An attacker injects malicious code or commands into input fields, which are then processed by the application without proper sanitization, leading to data corruption or unauthorized access.
mitigations: Implement strict input validation and sanitization for all user inputs.
Use parameterized queries or prepared statements to prevent SQL injection.
Employ a Web Application Firewall (WAF) to filter malicious requests.
